pr65.title: Readonly or mutable engine as per user configuration
pr65.body:
## Summary

Implements **R-1 / S-1** from the issue #47 case study: a read-only or mutable
engine selected by user configuration. Today `LinkNetwork` is always mutable;
this adds an enforced read-only mode that composes with existing snapshot
sharing instead of duplicating it.

Fixes #49.

## What changed

- **`AccessMode { Mutable, ReadOnly }`** on `ParseConfiguration`
  (`with_access_mode` / `access_mode`). Defaults to `Mutable`, so existing
  callers see no behavior change.
- **`ReadOnlyNetwork`** — a frozen view produced by `LinkNetwork::freeze()` /
  `as_read_only()`. It derefs to `&LinkNetwork`, exposing every non-mutating
  operation (query, project, reconstruct, verify, serialize) while leaving the
  `&mut self` mutators *unreachable at compile time* (there is no `DerefMut`).
  The network is held behind an `Arc`, so clones share one allocation.
- **`EngineNetwork`** + **`LinkNetwork::parse_engine()`** — the runtime
  boundary that honours the configured access mode. Read-only parsing returns
  the frozen form, and `EngineNetwork::as_mutable()` rejects mutation with a
  clear `ReadOnlyViolation` diagnostic.
- **Snapshot interop** — `NetworkSnapshot::as_read_only()` /
  `from_read_only()` reuse the snapshot's `Arc<LinkNetwork>`, so freezing and
  snapshot versioning share one allocation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [x] `ParseConfiguration` carries an access mode; default remains `Mutable`.
- [x] A read-only view supports every non-mutating public operation and none of
      the mutating ones (compile-time enforced via `Deref`-without-`DerefMut`).
- [x] Tests cover: freeze → query/reconstruct/verify works; mutation is
      impossible (`compile_fail` doctest) and rejected at the engine boundary
      (`ReadOnlyViolation`); snapshot interop; `Arc` sharing.
- [x] Changelog fragment added (`bump: minor`).

## How to verify

```bash
cargo test --all-features access_mode      # unit tests
cargo test --all-features --doc access     # passing + compile_fail doctests
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features
cargo fmt --check
```

Read-only usage:

```rust
use meta_language::{AccessMode, LinkNetwork, ParseConfiguration};

let configuration = ParseConfiguration::default().with_access_mode(AccessMode::ReadOnly);
let mut engine = LinkNetwork::parse_engine("alpha beta", "plain-text", configuration);

assert!(engine.is_read_only());
assert_eq!(engine.reconstruct_text(), "alpha beta"); // reads work via Deref
assert!(engine.as_mutable().is_err());               // mutation rejected
```

pr66.title: feat: adopt data-exchange format grammars (JSON, YAML, TOML, XML, INI, protobuf, GraphQL)
pr66.body:
## Summary

Closes #50 — adopts data-exchange / interchange format grammars by wiring seven compatible tree-sitter grammar crates through `src/tree_sitter_adapter.rs`, following the PR #44–#46 pattern.

Each format parses through `LinkNetwork::parse`, emits real `LinkType::Syntax` concrete-syntax links, and reconstructs the source **byte-for-byte**.

### Grammars wired

| Format | Labels (case-insensitive) | Crate | Version | Root node |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| JSON | `JSON` | `tree-sitter-json` | 0.24.8 | `document` |
| YAML | `YAML`, `yml` | `tree-sitter-yaml` | 0.7.2 | `stream` |
| TOML | `TOML` | `tree-sitter-toml-ng` | 0.7.0 | `document` |
| XML | `XML` (+ `DTD`) | `tree-sitter-xml` | 0.7.0 | `document` |
| INI | `INI` | `tree-sitter-ini` | 1.4.0 | `document` |
| Protocol Buffers | `protobuf`, `proto`, `Protocol Buffers` | `tree-sitter-proto` | 0.4.0 | `source_file` |
| GraphQL | `GraphQL`, `gql` | `tree-sitter-graphql` | 0.1.0 | `source_file` |

All seven use the modern `tree-sitter-language` ABI binding, so they link against the project's `tree-sitter 0.25.8` front end.

### Registry & fixtures

- `DATA_FORMAT_TARGETS` registry + new `LanguageFamily::DataFormat` in `src/parity.rs`, gated by parity tests mirroring `MARKUP_LANGUAGE_TARGETS`.
- One UTF-8 `LANGUAGE_FIXTURES` round-trip entry per format (with accented/non-ASCII content where applicable).
- `tests/unit/grammar_parsing.rs`: grammar-backed round-trip + syntax-link + root-node assertions, case-insensitive alias coverage, a **JSON recovery** case, and a **JSON-in-Markdown-fence mixed-region** test (a ` ```json ` fence parses into the host links network).
- `tests/unit/link_network.rs`: `data_format_targets_cover_interchange_format_scope` pins the exact target set and asserts CSV/JSON5 are absent.

### CSV / JSON5 deferral (no silent gap)

`docs/parity-roadmap.md` gains a **Data-Exchange Format Coverage** section documenting the seven grammars (crate/version/license/root node) and an explicit CSV/JSON5 deferral: both `tree-sitter-csv` 1.2.0 and `tree-sitter-json5` 0.1.0 still pin `tree-sitter ~0.20` as published, incompatible with the 0.25 toolchain. Tracked for a follow-up once compatible bindings are published or vendored.

## How to reproduce / verify

- `cargo test --all-features` — **120 passed**.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` and `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` — clean.
- Gate scripts pass: `check-no-src-tests`, `check-file-size`, `check-changelog-fragment` (1 fragment, `bump: minor`).
- Throwaway verification scripts kept under `experiments/`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [x] Each format parses/reconstructs byte-for-byte.
- [x] `DATA_FORMAT_TARGETS` gated by tests.
- [x] At least one mixed-region fixture embeds a data format inside Markdown.
- [x] Roadmap documents CSV/JSON5 handling — no silent gap.
- [x] Changelog fragment added (`bump: minor`).

issue49.title: Readonly or mutable engine as per user configuration

issue50.title: Adopt data-exchange format grammars (JSON, YAML, TOML, XML, INI, protobuf, GraphQL)
